description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The stone appears to have a rough, slightly mottled gray texture, suggesting it is made from a durable material such as granite or concrete. The sides of the block are smooth and solid black, contrasting with the textured top. The block is thick and robust, likely designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its overall appearance is utilitarian, suitable for use in public spaces where durability and functionality are prioritized.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its gray, rough-textured top and smooth black sides. The striped pattern and solid build suggest it is used as a durable urban barrier.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the grooved top, showing the full width of the parallel stripes and the solid black front edge.
description_view_1: Viewed from the side, the grooves appear as long, deep channels running from front to back, emphasizing the striped pattern.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this angle shows the grooved top surface with the stripes running horizontally and the solid black edge below.
description_view_3: From this side, the grooves are again prominent, running the full depth of the block, with the black side edge visible.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or directly on a leveled surface
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no signs of erosion or moss.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
